title: Find Common Free Time of Employees
description:
You are given schedules for K employees, where each schedule is a list of disjoint, sorted closed intervals representing working time. Return the list of finite, nonempty intervals representing time where all employees are free. Assume time is on the real line.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ K ≤ 100
- Total intervals ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
Flatten all working intervals across employees and merge them to get a global busy timeline. The gaps between consecutive merged busy blocks are exactly the common free times for all employees.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Collect all intervals and sort by start
2. Main Algorithm: Merge overlaps into a single busy list; while merging, whenever a gap appears between the previous end and the next start, emit that gap as free time
3. Return Result: Return all gaps

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(N log N) for sorting
- Space Complexity: O(N) for merged lists

Key Insights
- Union of busy intervals defines complement as free time
- Only finite gaps between merged blocks count

Common Pitfalls
- Do not return gaps before first start or after last end
- Schedule lists may be unbalanced in size—flatten first

testCases.explanation: Touching times give no open gap
No working intervals means no finite common gaps

hints: Gaps between merged blocks are common free time
Ignore infinite gaps outside the convex hull of busy time
